mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced household consumption, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand contraction, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and negative market returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >65% of months where both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent annualized during high inflation periods signals capacity constraints and input cost inflation, compressing profit margins and driving negative returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns follow strong industrial production growth during periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index breaching the 305 threshold triggers immediate market repricing for aggressive central bank tightening, causing sharp negative forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 305.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, mechanically supporting positive equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in >60% of months with positive month-over-month payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where quarterly GDP growth decelerates while monthly retail sales accelerate signals inventory drawdown and an impending restocking cycle, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns are negative in >65% of periods when GDPC1 growth slows alongside RSAFS acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index falling below the 100 baseline signals severe supply-side contraction and recession risk, triggering risk-off flows and negative market returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns follow INDPRO dropping below 100 in >60% of triggered instances.
